title: scaevola eneabba
intro: Scaevola eneabba, also known as 'Blue Fan Flower', is a flowering plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its vibrant blue flowers and delicate foliage. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of the Scaevola eneabba plant have been used in Aboriginal medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and fever. The plant is believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Scaevola eneabba is often used in teas, salves, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
